properties.name: Mission Santa Ynez
properties.summary: Founded 1804. Zalvidea departure point.

properties.name: Mission San Gabriel
properties.summary: Founded 1771. Zalvidea arrival.

properties.name: Mission San Juan Bautista
properties.summary: Founded 1797. Moraga departure point.

properties.name: Mission San Fernando
properties.summary: Founded 1797. Moraga arrival.

properties.name: Jonatas
properties.summary: At Las Olivas

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Saca
properties.summary: On Alamo Pintado Cr.

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Olomosong
properties.summary: On Sisquoc R.

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Geep
properties.summary: South slope Sierra Madre

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Talhulimit
properties.summary: 2 leagues east of Geep

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Lisahua
properties.summary: Salisbury Canyon

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Sgene
properties.summary: Lower Cuyama Valley

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Malapoa
properties.summary: Bitterwater/Santiago Cr.; Tlamini

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Sispetis (Pohatan Tinliu)
properties.summary: SE corner Buena Vista Lake

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Tupal
properties.summary: Tejon Ranch, El Paso Cr.

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Tacui
properties.summary: Tecuya Creek

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Yagueleme (Woilo)
properties.summary: Kern R. / Kern Lake

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Tashlpj
properties.summary: Pastoria / Grapevine Cr.

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Taulames (Jahulamne)
properties.summary: Between Tuolumne & Stanislaus; deserted by 1806

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Pizcache
properties.summary: On the San Joaquin, near Le Grand

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Pitkachi
properties.summary: South side San Joaquin; Kohou near Herndon

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Telame (Telamni)
properties.summary: Kaweah delta; 600 souls; 22 baptized Oct. 1806

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

properties.name: Mission Santa Ynez
properties.summary: Departure, July 20, 1806.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

properties.name: Jonatas — Las Olivas
properties.summary: First stop north of mission, at Las Olivas (present-day Los Olivos area).

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

properties.name: Saca — Alamo Pintado Creek
properties.summary: Village of Saca on Alamo Pintado Creek.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

properties.name: Olomosong — Sisquoc River
properties.summary: Probably on the Sisquoc R. near the 120th meridian.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1: 'After 4 leagues further travel')

properties.name: Geep — South Slope, Sierra Madre
properties.summary: Apparently on the south slope of the Sierra Madre range. After climbing a mountain they came out onto the Cuyama Valley plains. T 10 N, R 28 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

properties.name: Talhulimit
properties.summary: Two leagues east of Geep, in the Cuyama Valley.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

properties.name: Lisahua — Salisbury Canyon
properties.summary: Probably in lower Salisbury Canyon. T 9 N, R 26 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 2)

properties.name: Camp — Lower Santa Barbara Canyon
properties.summary: In lower Santa Barbara Canyon. T 9 N, R 25 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note (cont.))

properties.name: Sgene — Lower Cuyama Valley
properties.summary: Party bore more to the north and found Sgene. T 10 N, R 25 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 3)

properties.name: Malapoa — Bitterwater / Santiago Creek
properties.summary: Located by Gifford & Schenck on Bitterwater or Santiago Cr. Identified as Tlamini (Kroeber).

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 4)

properties.name: Sispetis — SE Buena Vista Lake
properties.summary: Considered to be Pohatan Tinliu, at the SE corner of Buena Vista Lake.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 4: Kroeber (1925, pl. 47))

properties.name: Buenasvista — 8 Leagues North
properties.summary: Eight leagues north of Bitterwater/Santiago Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 5)

properties.name: Tejon / El Paso Creek — Foothills
properties.summary: Six leagues from Sispetis to the mouth of Tejon or El Paso Cr. at the edge of the foothills. July 26 reconnaissance explored El Paso, Tejon, and Pastoria creeks.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 6)

properties.name: Tupal — Tejon Ranch on El Paso Creek
properties.summary: Placed doubtfully by Gifford & Schenck at Tejon Ranch on El Paso Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 6)

properties.name: Tacui — Tecuya Creek
properties.summary: Party doubled back west past Grapevine Cr. to Tacui, undoubtedly on Tecuya Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 7)

properties.name: Yagueleme — Kern River / Woilo
properties.summary: Nine leagues north of Tecuya, on the Kern R. Gifford & Schenck identify as Woilo, very close to 9 leagues from Tecuya.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 8)

properties.name: Eastern End of Kern Lake
properties.summary: The eastern end of Kern Lake.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 9)

properties.name: Tashlpj — Pastoria / Grapevine Creek
properties.summary: Cook thinks more likely Pastoria on Grapevine Cr. rather than Tejon Cr., since the party arrived at Castaic at the head of Grapevine Cr. on August 7.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 10)

properties.name: Castaic — Head of Grapevine Creek
properties.summary: Party arrived August 7. Beginning the crossing south.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

properties.name: Antelope Valley
properties.summary: Crossed into Antelope Valley.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 11)

properties.name: San Gabriel Mountains Crossing
properties.summary: Party crossed the mountains and went southwest. Several villages of Serrano Indians seen.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 12)

properties.name: Mission San Gabriel
properties.summary: Arrival at San Gabriel Mission.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 12)

properties.name: Mission San Juan Bautista
properties.summary: Departure, late September 1806. Fr. Pedro Muñoz accompanying as chaplain.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

properties.name: San Benito River Camp
properties.summary: 1½ leagues from the mission, on the San Benito R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 13: 'not on Pacheco Cr., as stated by Cutter')

properties.name: San Luis Ranch — Pacheco Pass
properties.summary: Camp approximately at San Luis Ranch, where Highway 152 crosses San Luis Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 14)

properties.name: Santa Rita Camp
properties.summary: 15 to 20 mi east of San Luis Gonzaga. Near Salt Slough or Paso Slough, 5–6 mi west of the main San Joaquin.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 15: 'The sloughs and channels of the San Joaquin are difficult to locate with precision on a modern map')

properties.name: Camp on the San Joaquin
properties.summary: On the San Joaquin R. T 8 S, R 11 E.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 16: villages effaced so thoroughly that modern informants gave no indication they ever existed)

properties.name: Bear Creek
properties.summary: Rather than Mariposa Cr. Approximately 3 leagues south of the Merced R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 18: 'Cutter thinks this was Bear Cr... I see no reason to disagree')

properties.name: Merced River — West of Livingston
properties.summary: Party which went north reached the Merced R. somewhere west of Livingston, probably at T 6 S, R 11 E.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 19)

properties.name: Dry Creek
properties.summary: Undoubtedly Dry Cr. 'The description is valid even today.'

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 21)

properties.name: Tuolumne River Area
properties.summary: Villages seen on the Tuolumne but were deserted. Oak forest environment.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook notes 20, 24: Taulames village between the Tuolumne and Stanislaus)

properties.name: Stanislaus River — Near Modesto
properties.summary: Remnants of the oak forest can still be seen, extending from the junction with the San Joaquin eastward. Both sides of the river.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 22: 'if it continued in a northwesterly direction from near Modesto')

properties.name: Upper Stanislaus — Knights Ferry Area
properties.summary: Six leagues upstream. Vicinity of Ripon, Riverbank, or Knights Ferry. Limestone bluffs, precipitous. The best guess is the vicinity of Knights Ferry.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 23)

properties.name: Calaveras River
properties.summary: First stream (Rio de ... Franciscos) identified as the Calaveras.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 25: 'There is little reason to disagree')

properties.name: Cosumnes River — Northernmost Point
properties.summary: Second stream identified as the Cosumnes. Linguistic border between Miwok and Maidu. The diarist fails to mention the Mokelumne.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 25: northernmost extent of the expedition)

properties.name: Cosumnes River — Turn South
properties.summary: Party turns south, staying close to the eastern edge of the valley along the Sierra foothills.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

properties.name: Stanislaus / Tuolumne Area (return)
properties.summary: Passing back through the Stanislaus and Tuolumne country.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

properties.name: Chowchilla / Mariposa Creek
properties.summary: Santo Domingo identified as Mariposa Cr.; Tecolote as the Chowchilla (Cutter).

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 26)

properties.name: Fresno River (Santa Ana)
properties.summary: The Santa Ana was the Fresno R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 27)

properties.name: San Joaquin at Pizcache
properties.summary: Pizcache on the San Joaquin, listed in the appendix. Moraga probably halted approximately north of Fresno, below Le Grand. Distinct absence of permanent Indian settlements on seasonal streams.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 27)

properties.name: Pitkachi Village Area
properties.summary: Pitkachi 'held the south side of the San Joaquin, living at Kohou, near Herndon or Sycamore; at Weshiu, on a slough; and at Gewachu, still farther downstream.' Village was in the mountains or at least the foothills.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 28: Gayton / Kroeber disagree on exact location)

properties.name: Kings River — Near Sanger
properties.summary: Kings R. was reached near Sanger or Centerville.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 31)

properties.name: Kaweah Delta — Visalia / Telame
properties.summary: 28th day: scouts reached the great oak forest along the Kaweah delta at or above Visalia. 29th day (Oct. 19): main party arrived. The large village of 600 souls — Telame (the Telamni). 22 persons baptized.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 33: 'The water evidently was very low — somewhat unusual for this region, even in October')

properties.name: Tule River — Near Porterville
properties.summary: After a winding course for two days, the party camped on the Tule R. near the foothills, probably not far from Porterville.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 36)

properties.name: Deer Creek
properties.summary: Probably Deer Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 37)

properties.name: White River
properties.summary: Probably White R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 38)

properties.name: Poso Creek
properties.summary: No distances given, but from the description the most likely stream is Poso Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 39)

properties.name: Kern River — Above Bakersfield
properties.summary: The Kern R., probably some miles above Bakersfield. A 'long trip' downstream brought them into the slough country south of Bakersfield.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 40)

properties.name: Kern River — Downstream / Slough Country
properties.summary: South of Bakersfield. Moraga did not get within sight of Buena Vista Lake, to the west.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook notes 40–41)

properties.name: Grapevine Canyon
properties.summary: Zalvidea had left an account of the villages in this area from his Aug. 1806 expedition.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 41)

properties.name: Tejon Pass
properties.summary: On the last three days the party left the valley by way of Grapevine Canyon, over Tejon Pass.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 42: 'Cutter says the Santa Susanna Range')

properties.name: Mission San Fernando
properties.summary: Arrival at San Fernando Mission. End of the 1806 expedition.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 42)
